structure_notes: PDF is a 9-column tabular document exported from Microsoft Excel 2010 (per metadata).
Document title (from page 1): 'Laxree Hotel Amenities SSP W.e.f. 1.03.2026'.
Column headers: ['Product', 'Product Category', 'Model No', 'Images', 'Description', 'Color', 'SSP', 's s o', 'AA R C P']
Organization = Category -> Tier (Essential/Premium/Lux) -> Products. Tier is stored in the 'Product Category' column and is forward-filled within each tier section.
The 7th column 'SSP' holds the price as a bare number (INR), e.g. '6700'. No currency symbol.
The 8th and 9th columns ('s/s/o', 'AA/R/C/P') are vertical-text labels and are EMPTY in all data rows — they appear to be page-margin labels (likely 'SSP' and 'As Above' or similar), not real fields.
3-tier classification CONFIRMED: Essential, Premium, Luxury (abbreviated 'Lux' in the tier column).
Total product rows in PDF: 222. Counts per tier: {'Essential': 55, 'Premium': 94, 'Luxury': 73}.
PDF is a curated SUBSET (195 unique codes) of the OLD XLSX master list (489 codes). 154 of 195 PDF codes also exist in the XLSX (79.0%).
